steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the 8 angles formed by the transversal
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, at each intersection it creates 4 angles.

Vertical angles are equal, so we get 2 pairs of equal angles at each intersection.

Because the lines are parallel, corresponding angles are equal—meaning both intersections have the same pair of angle measures.

Let the acute angle measure be $\alpha$ and the obtuse angle measure be $180 - \alpha$.

This gives us 4 angles of measure $\alpha$ (acute) and 4 angles of measure $180 - \alpha$ (obtuse).

steps.title: List all possible sums of 4 angles
steps.content:
From 8 angles (4 acute, 4 obtuse), we can select any 4. The five possible sums are:
  • All 4 acute: $4\alpha$
  • All 4 obtuse: $\left(4\right)(180 - \alpha) = 720 - 4\alpha$
  • 3 acute, 1 obtuse: $3\alpha + (180 - \alpha) = 2\alpha + 180$
  • 2 acute, 2 obtuse: $2\alpha + 2(180 - \alpha) = 360$
  • 1 acute, 3 obtuse: $\alpha + 3(180 - \alpha) = 540 - 2\alpha$

steps.title: Substitute the given angle and evaluate both cases
